title_slide: The Cost of Carrying Things Nobody Sees

slides.heading: The Weight Nobody Sees
bullet_points: Not burnout from doing too much
Exhaustion from holding too much
No metric captures it
slides.speaker_note: Open by naming the gap between what we measure and what we actually carry.

slides.heading: The Cognitive Tax
bullet_points: Unresolved things run background processes
Zeigarnik effect: mind holds incomplete loops
Emotional weight draws from the same pool as work
slides.speaker_note: Introduce the psychological mechanism — this is why invisible weight shows up in your output.

slides.heading: What It Looks Like
bullet_points: Rereading the same paragraph three times
Decisions feel heavier than they should
40 minutes pass with nothing to show
slides.speaker_note: Make it concrete — these familiar symptoms rarely get connected to emotional load.

slides.heading: It Doesn't Stay Invisible
bullet_points: Leaks into relationships without warning
The half-second delay before warmth
Silence has a texture people can feel
slides.speaker_note: Shift from internal cost to relational cost — the invisible load is visible to those who love you.

slides.heading: Why We Keep Carrying
bullet_points: Putting it down means looking at it first
The weight becomes familiar — even safe
Letting go can feel like betrayal
slides.speaker_note: Address the real reason people don't stop — it's not fear of judgment, it's the cost of looking.

slides.heading: The First Move
bullet_points: Not a framework or a breakthrough
Name it accurately — even only to yourself
The loop needs attention, not resolution
slides.speaker_note: Give a non-prescriptive entry point — acknowledgment as the one starting action.

slides.heading: Stop Calling It Nothing
bullet_points: You don't have to share it
You don't have to resolve it today
You have to let it be what it is
slides.speaker_note: End on the core message — not a solution, but a shift in how you hold what you're carrying.
